REFERENCE: The reference contains multiple fabricated elements: the journal name 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is not a real journal; the volume number '527' is implausibly high; the article title is a direct copy of a real paper published in The Lancet, and the DOI corresponds to a different paper entirely. The author list 'Haouz A, Wongwiwat W, Mongkolsapaya J, Screaton GR, Dai X, Sharma A, et al.' does not match the attributed study.
authors: The listed authors do not correspond to the real study by NCD Risk Factor Collaboration (NCD-RisC) published in The Lancet. The author set appears fabricated or mismatched.
title: Symptoms: The title 'Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014: a pooled analysis of 1698 population-based measurement studies with 19·2 million participants' matches a real 2016 Lancet paper by NCD-RisC. However, it is incorrectly attributed here to fabricated authors and journal.
journal_name: See above; the journal name is entirely fabricated.
journal_volume: Volume '527' is not correct for The Lancet; the real volume is 387 for the 2016 BMI trends paper.
journal_issue: 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is not a recognized journal; 'Br' is an abbreviation for British, but 'Hawmatol' and 'Mol' are nonsensical. The real journal for this study is The Lancet.
journal_page: The page range is not explicitly given, but the elocator '527(12146)' is invalid. The real paper has pages 1377–1396 in The Lancet volume 387.
elocator: The elocator '527(12146)' does not correspond to any real publication. For the genuine 2016 Lancet paper, the volume is 387 (issue 10026), with pages 1377–1396. The volume '527' is used by Nature (e.g., 2015), not The Lancet.
publication_date: The date '2018 Aug 20' is incorrect. The real study was published online on March 31, 2016, and in print in April 2016.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9' is associated with a different publication: a 2009 article in The Lancet about global height trends, not the 2016 BMI trends paper. It is incorrectly assigned.
pmcid: Not provided in the reference; cannot be evaluated.
pmid: Not provided; the reference lacks a PMID.

REFERENCE: The citation is a 'hallucination' or 'chimera' that mixes structural biology authors, a global health title, a non-existent journal subtitle, and an unrelated DOI.
authors: The authors listed are structural biologists (e.g., Felix Rey, A. Haouz), while the provided title is a global health study traditionally authored by the NCD Risk Factor Collaboration.
title: The title refers to a famous NCD Risk Factor Collaboration study published in The Lancet in 2016, not 2018.
journal_name: British Journal of Haematology exists, but 'Molceulwr' is a misspelling and not part of the official title; also, this journal does not publish global BMI pooled analyses.
journal_volume: The volume 527 is inconsistent with the provided date and the actual publication volume of the cited title.
journal_issue: Issue number 12146 is highly atypical for a medical journal and does not match the actual publication details of the study.
journal_page: No page numbers were provided in the citation string beyond the volume/issue segment.
elocator: No specific elocator was provided, and the issue number appears to be acting as a placeholder.
publication_date: The actual Lancet paper for this title was published in 2016, not 2018.
doi: The DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9 actually resolves to an unrelated 2009 Lancet article about global health, not the BMI study.
pmcid: No PMCID was provided in the source text to evaluate.
pmid: No PMID was provided in the source text to evaluate.

REFERENCE: This is a **fabricated/hybrid citation** that combines multiple mismatched elements. The title ('Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries...') is from a well-known 2016 Lancet paper by the NCD Risk Factor Collaboration, but the author list clearly consists of virologists/structural biologists (e.g., Screaton, Rey, DEjnirattisai, Zhou ZH) known for work on flaviviruses (dengue, Zika). The journal name 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is nonsensical and not a real journal. The DOI belongs to the original Lancet BMI study. The PMID (33714382) and PMCID (PMC9462833) do not correspond to this reference. The publication date (Aug 20) and volume/issue numbering (527(12146)) are inconsistent with any real publication.
authors: The authors listed are real researchers but are primarily virologists and structural biologists (e.g., Screaton GR, Rey FA, Zhou ZH, DEjnirattisai W), not public health/epidemiology researchers who authored the Lancet BMI paper. The combination of these specific authors with this BMI title is fabricated.
title: The title 'Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014: a pooled analysis of 1698 population-based measurement studies with 19·2 million participants' is a real title, but it belongs to a 2016 Lancet paper by the NCD Risk Factor Collaboration, not to the authors listed here.
journal_name: The journal name 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is not a real journal abbreviation. It appears to be a fabricated or corrupted name, possibly mimicking 'British Journal of Haematology' but with a nonsensical suffix 'Hawmatol'.
journal_volume: The volume '527' is not a standard journal volume and is not consistent with The Lancet or any verifiable publication.
journal_issue: The journal name 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is not a real journal. It may be a garbled version of 'British Journal of Haematology' or 'British Journal of Molecular Biology', but neither of those published this BMI paper. The issue number '527(12146)' is also unrecognizable.
elocator: The e-locator '527(12146)' does not correspond to a recognized format for any real journal. It is not consistent with The Lancet or any other standard journal citation.
publication_date: The publication date '2018 Aug 20' does not match the Lancet BMI paper (which was published in 2016) or any known publication by the listed authors with this title.
doi: The DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9 appears to be related to the Lancet (S0140-6736), possibly from a 2009 paper, but the year in the DOI prefix ((09)) does not match the 2018 publication date given. It may be a misattributed or corrupted DOI string.
pmcid: The PMCID 'PMC9462833' does not correspond to the actual Lancet BMI paper. It may belong to a different publication entirely.
pmid: The PMID '33714382' does not correspond to the BMI trends paper. A check of PubMed would reveal this PMID points to a different or unrelated publication.

REFERENCE: This reference contains multiple fabricated or incorrect elements. While the title closely matches a real paper (with minor differences), the authors are completely wrong (the real author is NCD-RisC, a collaborative group), the journal name is nonsensical, the volume and issue numbers are incorrect, the date is wrong, and the DOI is incorrect. The reference appears to be a heavily corrupted or fabricated version of the real NCD-RisC 2016 Lancet paper on BMI trends.
authors: The author list provided does not match the actual authors of the paper 'Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014'. The actual authors are the NCD Risk Factor Collaboration (NCD-RisC), which would be the corporate author. The names listed (Haouz A, Wongwiwat W, Mongkolsapaya J, Screaton GR, Dai X, Sharma A) appear to be fabricated or incorrectly assigned. While some of these names might be real researchers in other fields (e.g., Screaton is a known immunologist), they are not the authors of this specific paper.
title: The title 'Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014: a pooled analysis of 1698 population-based measurement studies with 19·2 million participants' appears to be a slightly altered version of the actual NCD-RisC paper published in The Lancet in 2016. The actual title is 'Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014: a pooled analysis of 1698 population-based measurement studies with 19·2 million participants.' This matches closely with the real title.
journal_name: The journal name 'Br J Hawmatol Mol' is nonsensical and not a real journal name. It appears to be a corrupted or fabricated version of 'British Journal of Haematology and Molecular Medicine' or similar, but this is not the correct journal for this paper. The actual journal is The Lancet.
journal_volume: The volume number '527(12146)' does not match the actual publication details. The paper was published in The Lancet in 2016, volume 387, issue 10026, pages 1377-1396. The numbers 527 and 12146 do not correspond to the correct volume or issue.
journal_issue: Issue number '12146' appears to be an invalid or fabricated issue number. The actual issue number for this paper is 10026.
journal_page: No page numbers are provided. The actual paper spans pages 1377-1396 in The Lancet.
elocator: The DOI provided '10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9' does not match the actual DOI for this paper. The actual DOI is 10.1016/S0140-6736(16)30054-X. The provided DOI contains the year prefix '09' which suggests it might correspond to a 2009 paper, but this paper was published in 2016. The structure looks like a Lancet DOI but with incorrect year and article identifier.
publication_date: The publication date '2018 Aug 20' is incorrect. The actual publication date is December 2016 (online) and 2017 (print) in The Lancet volume 387, issue 10026.
doi: The DOI '10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9' is not the correct DOI for this paper. The actual DOI is 10.1016/S0140-6736(16)30054-X. The format resembles a Lancet DOI structure but with incorrect year (09 vs 16) and article identifier (60658-9 vs 30054-X).
pmcid: No PMCID is provided in the reference. The actual PMCID for this paper is PMC4879161.
pmid: No PMID is provided in the reference. The actual PMID for this paper is 27115820.

REFERENCE: Multiple essential bibliographic elements (journal name, volume, issue, pages, date, DOI, PMID/PMCID) are erroneous or missing. While the title is correct and the author list contains some plausible names, the overall reference does not correspond to any real publication.
authors: While some surnames (e.g., Wongwiwat, Mongkolsapaya, Screaton, Dai, Sharma, Zhou, Rey, England, Dejnirattisai, Gong, Zhang, Duquerroy, Rouvinski, Vaney) resemble contributors to the large NCD‑Risk‑Factor‑Collaboration (NCD‑RisC) author list, the presented subset is incomplete, contains misspellings (e.g., “Haouz” instead of a known author such as “Haeusler” or “Häussler”), and does not match the actual collaborative author list for the cited paper. The true paper lists over a thousand contributors under the consortium name "NCD Risk Factor Collaboration (NCD‑RisC)".
title: The title exactly matches the known Lancet article: "Trends in adult body-mass index in 200 countries from 1975 to 2014: a pooled analysis of 1698 population-based measurement studies with 19·2 million participants". This part is authentic.
journal_name: The cited journal "British Journal of Haematolog y– Molceulwr" is garbled and does not correspond to any recognized journal. The true article appeared in *The Lancet*, not in a haematology‑focused periodical. Hence the journal name is fabricated.
journal_volume: Volume 527 does not exist for *The Lancet* in the relevant timeframe; the correct volume for the 2016 paper is 387. Therefore the volume number is incorrect.
journal_issue: Issue 12146 is not a valid issue number for *The Lancet*; the true issue is 10026 (June 25, 2016). Hence the issue is fabricated.
journal_page: Page numbers are omitted (the citation shows only the volume and issue). The genuine article occupies pages 1377‑1396. Lack of correct pagination further indicates inauthenticity.
elocator: No article number or e‑locator is provided; the real paper does not use an e‑locator format. This field is irrelevant/missing.
publication_date: No year, month, or day is given. The correct publication date is 25 June 2016. The absence of a date makes the citation incomplete and thus unauthentic.
doi: The DOI "10.1016/S0140-6736(09)60658-9" resolves to a different article (a 2009 Lancet paper on something else). The correct DOI for the BMI‑trends paper is "10.1016/S0140-6736(16)30140-9". Therefore the supplied DOI is incorrect.
pmcid: No PMCID is supplied; the article is available in PMC (PMC4924004). Missing identifier further indicates a fabricated or incomplete citation.
pmid: No PMID is supplied; the genuine article has PMID 27615622. Its absence contributes to the citation’s inauthenticity.
